name: Diego Hernandez

bio.text: I study why some people refuse a partisan label, and how that refusal translates into political behavior. My research brings the methods of political psychology to understand a group that most accounts of partisanship treat as a residual category: the American independent.

researchLede: In addition to my dissertation work, I have research interests in why some individuals say they believe in conspiracies when they do not behave like they truly believe them, and how that signaling behavior maps onto their politics.
dataTag: survey experiments, quantitative and qualitative methods

publications.title: Democratic Governance, Interpersonal Trust, and Interpersonal Responsibility: Do Values Predict Vote Choice in Hypothetical EU Membership Referenda?
Searching For Uniqueness in the Crowd: Conspiracies and their Believers

teachingLede: I teach comparative and European Union politics, and I build courses around the gap between how institutions are supposed to work and how citizens actually reason about them.

teaching.title: POL309: Politics of the European Union
teaching.meta: Instructor of Record · Summer 2025; Summer 2026; Fall 2026; Spring 2027

cvLede: A short version lives here. The full document, with all positions, presentations, and service, is in the PDF.

cvFields.title: Methods
cvFields.detail: Quantitative social science, data analysis in R, Python, and SPSS programming languages, social science experiments, Qualtrics; qualitative methods including focus groups and interviews, text-as-data approaches

contactLede: Email is the best way to reach me. I am happy to share materials, or replication code when appropriate.
galleryLede: The inspiration for the color themes and art on this website came from a 2019 trip to Vienna, where Klimt's gold, Hoffmann's squares, and ornament laid flat against the wall stayed with me. Each of these AI-generated pieces were prompted in Anthropic's Claude Sonnet and created by Midjourney. Pick one and hang it. The About page keeps your choice until you change it.

gallery.title: The Tree
gallery.desc: The original About background and the first in the series. A tree grows out of a pale gridded wall, its canopy built from raised amber and copper cubes, with dark mosaic water collecting below.

gallery.title: The Archive
gallery.desc: Rectangles stacked like folios seen edge-on. Umber and slate under matte varnish, with gold leaf catching a few exposed edges.

gallery.title: The River
gallery.desc: Two eddies turn against the current, banded in viridian and oxblood with threads of gold. Mosaic squares collect along the banks.

gallery.title: The Laurel
gallery.desc: Laurel branches flattened into ornament, antique gold and muted teal on a dark umber ground. The closest of these to a Wiener Werkstätte frieze.

gallery.title: The Currents
gallery.desc: A second river. The bands run looser here, crossed by hairlines of gold, with pale gold leaf above and a shore of small squares along the bottom.
